title: [InstCombine] Bail out on inner disjoint or in `foldSelectICmpEq`

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @test_commutative_xor_eq_c_or_not_c(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %xor = xor i32 %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %xor, 8
  %or = or i32 %x, %y
  %not_c = xor i32 8, -1
  %or_not = or i32 %or, %not_c
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %or, i32 %or_not
  ret i32 %cond
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary does not explicitly discuss commutativity of inner operations (xor %y, %x vs xor %x, %y) or the NotRHS/NotInner mask variations. The patch handles this via matchFalseVal but summary doesn't detail operand ordering flexibility.

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @test_commutative_xor_eq_c_or_not_c(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %xor = xor i32 %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %xor, 8
  %or = or i32 %x, %y
  %not_c = xor i32 8, -1
  %or_not = or i32 %or, %not_c
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %or, i32 %or_not
  ret i32 %cond
}

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_select_xor_eq_allones_and_xor_notc(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %xor = xor i32 %x, %y
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %xor, -1
  %and = and i32 %x, %y
  %not = xor i32 -1, -1
  %xor1 = xor i32 %and, %not
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %and, i32 %xor1
  ret i32 %cond
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_select_xor_eq_allones_and_xor_notc(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %xor = xor i32 %x, %y
  %and = and i32 %x, %y
  %xor1 = xor i32 %and, 0
  ret i32 %xor1
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions 'any constant C' but does not specifically call out edge cases like all-ones (-1) which may have special simplification opportunities

  %xor = xor i32 %x, %y
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %xor, -1
  %and = and i32 %x, %y
  %not = xor i32 -1, -1
  %xor1 = xor i32 %and, %not
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %and, i32 %xor1
  ret i32 %cond
}

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_select_xor_eq_minus1_notrhs_pattern(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %xor = xor i32 %x, %y
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %xor, -1
  %or = or i32 %x, %y
  %not_c = xor i32 -1, -1
  %and = and i32 %or, %not_c
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %or, i32 %and
  ret i32 %cond
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions NotRHS cases for bitwise negation but doesn't show C=-1 specifically. This tests the edge case where C is all-ones, triggering the NotRHS matching path not explicitly detailed.
